subject: Accounting
title: What Is Bookkeeping?
description: A short lesson to help you apply What Is Bookkeeping?.

hook.text: Every business transaction—every sale, purchase, and payment—needs to be written down. That's bookkeeping.

buildup.text: Bookkeeping is the systematic recording of financial transactions in a company's records.

discovery.text: It's the foundation of accounting—without accurate records, no financial statement can be trusted.

twist.text: Bookkeeping isn't accounting. Bookkeepers record. Accountants analyze, interpret, and report.

climax.text: Every billion-dollar company started with someone writing down what came in and what went out.

quiz.question: What is the primary role of bookkeeping?
